pr.title: Add audit_spec_mapping.py for chemistry spec mapping audits
pr.body: ## Summary Adds `scripts/audit_spec_mapping.py`, a read-first auditor for chemistry spec resolution and compliance gaps, aligned with the current schema and `v_chem_compliance` / `094` behavior. ## What it does - **Phase 1:** Aggregates from `v_chem_compliance_summary` (heat counts, `no_spec_count` / `missing_spec_count`, UNKNOWN grade heats). - **Phase 2:** Top `NO_SPEC` / `MISSING_SPEC` rows from `v_chem_compliance`, plus `grade_spec_map` and `chem_spec_code_alias` inventory. - **Phase 3:** Sample of old `chem_readings` with no `lot_tracking` row (18+ months by `COALESCE(tested_at, imported_at)`), with distinct-heat count. - **Phase 4:** `grade_spec_map.spec_code` values with no matching `chem_element_specs` row (case/trim normalized join). Optional flags (documented in the script header): `--fix` (runs `build_grade_spec_map.py`), `--delete-orphans` (narrow delete by `chem_readings.id`), `--refresh-alerts` (subprocess to `refresh_chem_compliance_alerts.py`), with `--yes` or interactive confirmation for destructive paths. ## Docs - `docs/chem_pipeline.md` — example invocations and a short note that **`chem_spec_code_alias` maps product spec strings** as used in the compliance view, not raw lab grade text, unless the view is changed. - `scripts/README.md` — one-line pointer under chemistry automation. ## Note on the earlier “Layer 1” grade→alias idea Bulk-inserting `(source_spec = grade, target_spec = spec)` from `grade_spec_map` does **not** match the current `094` join (`chem_spec_code_alias` is matched to `psc.spec_code`). The script docstring states this explicitly so operators do not expect that SQL to fix unmapped grades without a view change.
pr.author: schoenwetteradam
